resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample explains that reports analyze how DNA influences health, summarize results as a risk score or gauge, use percentile comparisons, and rank recommendations by impact and evidence.
resultRows.description: The introductory pages also state that reports do not diagnose health conditions and that genetics is only one part of health.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary says the report contains 23 genetic results and covers mental health, substance use and eating disorders, cognitive problems, and cognitive traits.
resultRows.description: The sample frames brain function as influenced by past experiences, current environment, diet, lifestyle, and DNA.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Overview of results / Mental Health
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible mental-health cards include Seasonal Low Mood typical likelihood; Stress typical likelihood; Anxiety typical likelihood; Caffeine-Related Anxiety less likely; Low Mood more likely; Mood Swings less likely; Obsessive-Compulsive Tendencies typical likelihood; Teeth Grinding less likely; Psychological Trauma typical likelihood.
resultRows.description: The sample overview uses lower/typical/higher likelihood labels and plain-English subtitles for each card.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample overview uses lower/typical/higher likelihood labels and plain-English subtitles for each card. This is sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. It is not a local Brain Health finding, diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, cognitive-status result, dementia-risk result, neurologic-risk result, treatment instruction, medication/supplement/therapy recommendation, school/work instruction, pediatric instruction, or actionability claim.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Overview of results / Addictions And Eating Disorders
resultRows.item: Five addictions and eating-disorder result cards
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible cards include Addictions typical likelihood; Alcohol Addiction less likely; Tobacco Addiction less likely; Cannabis Addiction less likely; Eating Disorders typical likelihood.
resultRows.description: The sample separates addiction/eating-disorder result cards from mental-health and cognitive cards.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Overview of results / Cognitive Problems and Traits
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible cognitive cards include Cognitive Decline typical likelihood; Brain Fog typical likelihood; Attention higher risk/more likely to have ADHD; Dyslexia more likely; Creativity higher; Memory Performance typical; Short-Term Memory typical; Processing Speed typical; Executive Function typical.
resultRows.description: The second overview page groups cognitive problems separately from cognitive traits.
genes: Cognitive problem and cognitive trait model cards
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The second overview page groups cognitive problems separately from cognitive traits. This is sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Risk score / Seasonal Low Mood
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Typical likelihood of seasonal affective disorder; 43rd percentile; based on 90 genetic variants.
resultRows.description: The sample page says genetics may account for about 30% of differences in seasonality and lists family history, higher latitude, and low vitamin D as other risk factors.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample page says genetics may account for about 30% of differences in seasonality and lists family history, higher latitude, and low vitamin D as other risk factors. It is not a local Brain Health finding, diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, cognitive-status result, dementia-risk result, neurologic-risk result, treatment instruction, medication/supplement/therapy recommendation, school/work instruction, pediatric instruction, or actionability claim.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Risk score / Stress
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Typical likelihood of feeling stressed; 38th percentile; based on 383,309 genetic variants.
resultRows.description: The sample page says up to 45% of differences in perceived stress may be attributed to genetics and explains HPA-axis stress-hormone context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample page says up to 45% of differences in perceived stress may be attributed to genetics and explains HPA-axis stress-hormone context. This is sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says recommendations are prioritized by likely impact based on genetics plus the amount of scientific evidence supporting the recommendation.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Recommendation / Relaxation Techniques
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible rows include Attention impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Low Mood impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Psychological Trauma impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Anxiety impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Brain Fog impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Addictions impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Stress impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Eating Disorders impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Executive Function impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Memory Performance impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Short-Term Memory impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Cognitive Decline impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Teeth Grinding impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Mood Swings impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Tobacco Addiction impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Alcohol Addiction impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Creativity impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Cannabis Addiction impact 3/5 evidence 3/5.
resultRows.description: The sample describes yoga, meditation, mindfulness, applied relaxation, massage, tai chi, biofeedback, and related practices across different rows.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample describes yoga, meditation, mindfulness, applied relaxation, massage, tai chi, biofeedback, and related practices across different rows. resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Recommendation / Relaxation Techniques
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says mindfulness-based relaxation may improve attention more in people with your ANKK1 gene variant.
resultRows.description: The visible variant table shows ANKK1 rs1800497 GG with a two-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The visible variant table shows ANKK1 rs1800497 GG with a two-dot evidence indicator. This is sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. It is not a local Brain Health finding, diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, cognitive-status result, dementia-risk result, neurologic-risk result, treatment instruction, medication/supplement/therapy recommendation, school/work instruction, pediatric instruction, or actionability claim.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Recommendation / Relaxation Techniques
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says people with your CELF4 gene variant may be more prone to stress and that relaxation techniques may help with PTSD by reducing stress.
resultRows.description: The visible variant table renders gene as '/', SNP rs11665070, genotype GA, with a two-dot evidence indicator.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Recommendation / Relaxation Techniques
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says your OXTR gene variant is associated with anxiety and issues with emotional attachment and that massage may help by boosting oxytocin.
resultRows.description: The visible variant table shows OXTR rs53576 AG with a two-dot evidence indicator.

neurobehavioralContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Brain Health is sample-backed for report structure, but this local example is still a boundary report: it can preserve sibling report sections and show missing evidence, not assess your brain health, current mental state, cognition, neurologic status, dementia risk, or treatment needs.

professionalReview: Do not use this local example to change medication, supplements, therapy, imaging, labs, school, work, pediatric, or treatment decisions.

Bring symptoms, family history, clinical history, medications, age, environment, cognitive or neurologic evaluations, and prior testing to a qualified professional if reviewing brain or neurobehavioral concerns.
Treat sibling sample rows as report-structure examples, not as personal findings.

No direct Brain Health mock-report body or sample report has been captured; sibling Mood & Brain sample rows are used only as output-structure evidence.
Unavailable model rows should never be interpreted as low risk, high ability, low ability, diagnosis, reassurance, or an all-clear result.
